title: EDHOC: __ASSERT used for crypto validation - silent failures in production
description:
SECURITY: Crypto operation return values are validated only with __ASSERT, which compiles to nothing in release builds (CONFIG_ASSERT=n).

Affected lines in edhoc.c:
- Lines 77, 80, 83: sha256_hash() - tc_sha256_* return values
- Lines 97, 100, 103, 106: hmac_sha256() - tc_hmac_* return values  
- Lines 147, 150, 154, 158, 161, 164: hkdf_expand() - tc_hmac_* return values

If any crypto operation fails in production, the function continues with uninitialized or corrupted output, potentially:
1. Using uninitialized memory as cryptographic keys
2. Producing predictable/weak keys
3. Causing authentication bypass

Fix: Replace __ASSERT with proper error checking that returns error codes. Crypto failures should propagate up to callers.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:77-164
